q: ___ is adiscipline of thermal engineering concerning the exchange of thermal energy between physical systems.
Which of the following is NOT one of the three basic ways of heat transfer?
In which state of matter is heat mainly transferred by convection?
Heat conduction involves energy transfer by ___ among adjacent parts.
What internally transfers heat via conduction in metals?
The rate of heat conduction is proportional to the area $A$ and ___.
The SI unit for thermal conductivity $k$ is ___.
___ indicates that the material is a poor thermal conductor.
What drives natural convection currents in a fluid?
Convection occurs in which of the following?
___ relates the rate of convection heat transfer to surface area and temperature difference.
Which mode of heat transfer is the fastest?
Thermal radiation is emitted by bodies because of their ___.
___ is an object that can absorb all electromagnetic radiations falling upon it.
A good absorber of thermal radiation is also a good ___.
The emissivity ($e$) of a white or silver coated object is ___.
___ deals with equilibrium states by outlining the relationship between energy and work.
Two systems are in thermal equilibrium when they are at the ___.
Which law provides a simple definition of thermodynamic equilibrium?
The change in internal energy $\\Delta U$ is equal to ___.
For an ideal gas, the internal energy depends only on ___.
___ is the sum of the energies of all constituent particles of a system.
A process that happens slowly enough for the system to remain in equilibrium is a ___ process.
Which process occurs at a constant pressure?
In which process is the work done by the system equal to zero?
In an isothermal process for an ideal gas, the change in internal energy is ___.
Which process has no heat transfer into or out of the system?
A heat engine is a system that converts heat into ___.
The ratio of work output to heat input is called ___.
The cycle of a gasoline engine is also called a ___ engine.
During which stroke is a mixture of gasoline vapour and air drawn into the cylinder?
___ stroke involves the hot combustion products expanding and pushing the piston out.
The diesel engine induces ___ into the combustion chamber on its intake stroke.
Who described a hypothetical engine known as the Carnot engine in 1824?
The Carnot engine operates on an ideal ___ cycle.
The Carnot cycle consists of ___ reversible processes.
The efficiency of a Carnot engine depends on the ___ of the heat reservoirs.
According to Carnot's theorem, all reversible engines operating between the same reservoirs are ___.
